title: Pfizer, Valneva Lyme Disease Vaccine Shows Over 70% Efficacy in Late-Stage Trial
summary: Bengaluru: The U.S. drugmaker Pfizer and France's Valneva said on ‚ÄčMonday their experimental ‚ÄčLyme disease vaccine showed more than 70% efficacy ‚Äčin a late-stage trial.Pfizer said it had planned to...
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
Bengaluru: The U.S. drugmaker Pfizer and France's Valneva said on ‚ÄčMonday their experimental ‚ÄčLyme disease vaccine showed more than 70% efficacy ‚Äčin a late-stage trial.

Pfizer said it had planned to seek U.S. regulatory approval for the vaccine called PF-07307405.

There is currently no ‚ÄĆapproved vaccine ‚Ā†to ‚Ā†prevent Lyme disease - spread by bites from infected blacklegged ticks - after ‚ÄčGSK discontinued its Lymerix shot in 2002, citing insufficient consumer demand.

While ‚Äčthe Pfizer-Valneva vaccine showed 73.2% efficacy from 28 days after the fourth dose, it missed the primary goal ‚Äčin the first analysis due to ‚Ā†fewer-than-anticipated Lyme ‚ÄĆcases being accrued in the trial leaving ‚Äča ‚Äčsmall number of data points.

However, a ‚Ā†second planned analysis met the bar, with 74.8% efficacy, ‚Äčadding to Pfizer's confidence "in the vaccine's potential," ‚Äčas it plans "submissions to regulatory authorities."

According to the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, symptoms of Lyme disease include fever, headache, fatigue, and a skin rash called erythema migrans. If left untreated, infection ‚ÄĆcan spread to joints, the heart, and the nervous system.

Most cases of Lyme disease can ‚Äčbe ‚Äčtreated successfully with a ‚Ā†few weeks of antibiotics.

The CDC estimates about 476,000 people are diagnosed and treated for Lyme annually in the U.S., ‚Äčwhile roughly 132,000 cases are reported each year in Europe, the companies said.

Last year, Valneva said it expects Pfizer to launch the vaccine in the second half of 2027 following approval.

Source : Reuters

Sheeba Farhat Joined Medical Dialogues in 2018 to report on the latest Education news. A Graduate of the University of Delhi, she specializes in covering stories related to Medical Education updates. For inquiries or further information, you can reach her at editorial@medicaldialogues.in.
